description: An I2C bus works at low speed but fails intermittently at higher speed because the pull-up resistors are too weak for the bus capacitance.
body: Failure mode: weak or absent external pull-ups leave the open-drain lines with slow rising edges that violate timing as the clock speeds up. Detection signals: rounded rising edges on a capture, errors that appear only at fast mode, and reliability that improves when the clock slows. Do not blame firmware for errors that track bus speed. Safer fallback: size the pull-ups for the bus capacitance and target rise time, verify edges on a scope, then retest at full speed.
